assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that on the Regular Channel, GKE will automatically upgrade the control plane and node pools to 1.31 at the EoS date.

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: The response explains that the Extended channel provides up to 24 months of support and does not hit EoS at the same time as the Regular channel.

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states that the control plane will be auto-upgraded and node pools will be force-upgraded even if legacy 'no auto-upgrade' settings are configured.

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: The response mentions that a strict 'no upgrades' exclusion (with a maximum of 30 days) can be used to delay the EoS force-upgrade.

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: The response advises taking control of the upgrade lifecycle before the EoS date and proactively scheduling the 1.30 to 1.31 upgrade.

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: The response suggests using the 'get-upgrade-info' gcloud command but does not mention GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging.

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: The deprecation insights dashboard is not mentioned anywhere in the response.

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss 'snowflaking' or explicitly warn against the risks of manually freezing versions.
